title: hymenophyllum myriocarpum
intro: Hymenophyllum myriocarpum, also known as the Many-fruited Rostellularia, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and prevent infections. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being. The plant is typically harvested and prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to be used in traditional medicine.
